standard_notation: pp -> T_{5/3} Tbar_{5/3} -> W+ t W- tbar
process_name: Pair production of exotic-charge T_{5/3} custodial top partner
implementation_difficulty_reason: A faithful LHC direct-search constraint needs event generation, detector-level efficiencies or recast likelihoods, branching-ratio scans, and/or external reinterpretation tools such as CheckMATE, MadAnalysis5, SModelS, or collaboration likelihood material.

commands: rg -n -e 'T5/3' -e 'T_\{5/3\}' -e 'X5/3' -e 'X_\{5/3\}' -e 'vector[-_ ]like' -e 'VLQ' -e 'top partner' -e 'same-sign' -e 'same charge' -e 'same-charge' -e 'collider' -e 'LHC' quarkConstraints qcd flavorConstraints neutrinos yukawa warpConfig solvers scanParams tests -g '!*.ipynb'

rg -n -e 'M_KK' -e 'MKK' -e 'Mkk' -e 'KK.*mass' -e 'mass.*KK' -e 'gstar' -e 'g_\*' -e 'g\*' -e 'Mmin' -e 'M_min' -e 'mKK' quarkConstraints qcd flavorConstraints neutrinos yukawa warpConfig solvers scanParams tests -g '!*.ipynb'

rg -n -e 'T53' -e 'Tprime' -e 'tprime' -e 't.?prime' -e 'Bprime' -e 'b.?prime' -e 'custodial' -e 'KK gluon' -e 'KK-gluon' -e 'resonance' quarkConstraints qcd flavorConstraints neutrinos yukawa warpConfig solvers scanParams tests -g '!*.ipynb'
findings: The collider-specific query returned no matches for T5/3, X5/3, VLQ, top-partner, collider, or LHC terms.
A broader query only found low-energy Delta F = 2 exclusion machinery and KK-gluon coupling/matching helpers.

Adjacent M_KK evidence: quarkConstraints/modern/scan.py:1230 maps Lambda_IR to M_KK for scan points; quarkConstraints/couplings.py:103-124 defines mass-basis KK-gluon couplings; quarkConstraints/modern/matching.py:240-243 states the matching bridge is not a full operator-complete EFT/RG package; quarkConstraints/deltaf2.py:1-10 describes the repo-owned Delta F = 2 exclusion slice.

contino_servant_2008.source_type: theory baseline for custodial T_5/3 same-sign dilepton searches; not a pdg_or_equivalent measured observable
contino_servant_2008.relevance: Identifies the charge-5/3 top partner as a custodial-parity signature and motivates the same-sign-dilepton Wt Wt topology.

mrazek_wulzer_2009.source_type: theory baseline for same-sign-dilepton top-partner reach; not a pdg_or_equivalent measured observable
mrazek_wulzer_2009.relevance: Emphasizes single production and same-sign dileptons in strong-sector top-partner searches.

rc11_quark_scan_context.observable: M_KK^{min,p50} at g_* = 3 for the live RUNA quark scan

atlas2018_single_production_coupling.source_type: model-assumption input for a single-production interpretation; not a measured observable
atlas2018_single_production_coupling.relevance: The stronger ATLAS same-charge-lepton interpretation including single production is quoted only for this coupling assumption.

post_2010_developments.why_matters: First LHC same-sign-dilepton charge-5/3 top-partner limit, assuming exclusive tW decay.
First CMS 13 TeV combination of same-sign dilepton and single-lepton final states for X_5/3.
ATLAS same-charge lepton search gave a pair-production T_5/3 interpretation and a separate coupling-dependent pair-plus-single interpretation.
CMS increased luminosity and combined same-sign dilepton with single-lepton channels, producing the strongest CMS pair-production limits in the PDG table.
Full Run-2 one-lepton plus MET search superseded the earlier pair-only T_5/3 mass reach for the exclusive B/X -> Wt interpretation.

sections.process:
Collider RS resonance / partner search framing: CR002 is the direct LHC
search for the exotic-charge custodial top partner that decays through
\(T_{5/3}\to W^+t\).  The process is
\[
  pp \to T_{5/3}\bar T_{5/3}
  \to W^+ t\, W^- \bar t ,
\]
equivalently \(pp\to X_{5/3}\bar X_{5/3}\) in CMS notation.  The distinctive
experimental handle is the same-sign dilepton or one-lepton plus jets topology
from the four \(W\) bosons in the \(t\bar tWW\) final state.
sections.pdg_prose:
The canonical PDG-equivalent entry is the current PDG encoder q009 mass limit,
\[
  m_{t'(5/3)} > 1460~\mathrm{GeV}\qquad(95\%~\mathrm{CL}),
\]
from the ATLAS full-Run-2 search in \(139~\mathrm{fb}^{-1}\) of
\(\sqrt{s}=13~\mathrm{TeV}\) data, for the exclusive \(B/X\to Wt\)
interpretation.  The same ATLAS paper quotes a stronger
\(1.59~\mathrm{TeV}\) lower limit for a mass-degenerate vector-like doublet,
which is a different branching-ratio and spectrum benchmark.

The strongest CMS pair-production limits in the PDG table are
\(m_{X_{5/3}}>1.33~\mathrm{TeV}\) for the right-handed coupling benchmark and
\(m_{X_{5/3}}>1.30~\mathrm{TeV}\) for the left-handed benchmark, using
\(35.9~\mathrm{fb}^{-1}\) at \(\sqrt{s}=13~\mathrm{TeV}\).  Earlier ATLAS
same-charge-lepton data gave \(m_{T_{5/3}}>1.19~\mathrm{TeV}\) for
pair production alone, and a coupling-dependent pair-plus-single-production
interpretation reached \(m_{T_{5/3}}>1.6~\mathrm{TeV}\).
sections.relevance:
CR002 is a direct LHC constraint on the custodial top-partner spectrum, not a
low-energy \(\Delta F=1\) or \(\Delta F=2\) observable.  It is therefore most
useful as a cross-check on non-anarchic or compressed custodial-RS spectra in
which the lightest fermion partners can be much lighter than the KK gauge
scale.  In an anarchic-flavor RS scan, the repo-local rc1.1 quark methodology
already reports
\[
  M_{KK}^{\min,p50}(\,g_\ast=3\,)=47.26~\mathrm{TeV},
\]
so a \(T_{5/3}\) direct-search reach near the \(1.5~\mathrm{TeV}\) scale is
not the leading constraint on the default anarchic-flavor ensemble.

The collider number constrains a physical custodial-partner mass under
specific LHC simplified-model assumptions.  Mapping it onto \(M_{KK}\) needs a
model-dependent relation between the fermion-partner spectrum, top
compositeness, and the gauge KK scale; it should not be treated as a direct
cut on the same \(M_{KK}\) variable used by the quark-flavor scan.
sections.post_2008:
The LHC program closely follows the Contino--Servant and Mrazek--Wulzer
same-sign-dilepton motivation:
\begin{itemize}
  \item CMS arXiv:1312.2391 established the first LHC same-sign-dilepton
  charge-\(5/3\) limit, excluding masses below \(800~\mathrm{GeV}\) at
  \(\sqrt{s}=8~\mathrm{TeV}\) for exclusive \(tW\) decay.
  \item CMS arXiv:1705.10967 moved the search to \(\sqrt{s}=13~\mathrm{TeV}\)
  and combined same-sign dilepton with single-lepton final states, reaching
  \(1.02~\mathrm{TeV}\) and \(0.99~\mathrm{TeV}\) for the right- and
  left-handed coupling benchmarks.
  \item ATLAS arXiv:1807.11883 used same-charge leptons and \(b\)-jets to set
  the \(1.19~\mathrm{TeV}\) pair-production limit and to demonstrate the
  stronger but coupling-dependent single-production interpretation.
  \item CMS arXiv:1810.03188 increased the dataset to \(35.9~\mathrm{fb}^{-1}\)
  and produced the \(1.33~\mathrm{TeV}\) / \(1.30~\mathrm{TeV}\) CMS Run-2
  limits.
  \item ATLAS arXiv:2212.05263 used the full Run-2 one-lepton plus
  missing-transverse-momentum search to provide the current PDG-leading
  \(1.46~\mathrm{TeV}\) exclusive \(B/X\to Wt\) limit.
\end{itemize}
sections.validity:
The pair-production cross section is QCD dominated, so the production rate is
less sensitive to the \(T_{5/3}Wt\) electroweak coupling than single
production.  The acceptance and the quoted mass limit are nevertheless tied to
the assumed prompt decay pattern, top-partner width, branching fractions, and
event topology.  If the \(T_{5/3}\) has sizable non-\(tW\) decays, cascade
decays into other custodial states, a broad width, or a compressed spectrum,
the simple PDG mass bound is not automatically applicable.  The stronger
single-production reinterpretation is explicitly coupling dependent and should
be catalogued separately from the pair-production CR002 entry if it is ever
implemented.
sections.implementation_difficulty_prose:
\textbf{HIGH.}  A faithful implementation would need at least a recast pipeline:
event generation for \(T_{5/3}\bar T_{5/3}\), decay and branching-ratio
control, detector-level efficiencies or public likelihoods, and validation
against ATLAS/CMS exclusion tables.  External reinterpretation tools such as
CheckMATE, MadAnalysis5, or SModelS would be more realistic than a
hand-written scan cut.
